perturbation.original_text: ABOUT CUSTOMER DATA" means information related specifically to About Customers, including but not limited to: (i) internet addresses and navigational information, including information disclosing the usage of Links within or available through the Insurance Channel; (ii) transactional information, including, but not limited to, names, addresses, billing information, information requested; and (iii) any other information that identifies an individual as an About Customer.
perturbation.changed_text: "ABOUT CUSTOMER DATA" means information related specifically to About Customers, including but not limited to: (i) internet addresses and navigational information, including information disclosing the usage of Links within or available through the Insurance Channel; (ii) transactional information, including, but not limited to, names, addresses, billing information, information requested, **excluding any financial details like credit card numbers or bank account information**; and (iii) any other information that identifies an individual as an About Customer.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by explicitly excluding financial details from 'About Customer Data'. This creates an in-text contradiction because later in section 9.1, Ebix is obligated to provide About with About Customer Data in aggregated form, which now creates uncertainty as to whether or not aggregated financial data needs to be shared, leading to a potential dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: eBix shall own and retain all right, title and interest in and to any About Customer data generated on the Insurance Center, and nothing in this Agreement shall confer in About any right, title or interest in the About Customer data (except to the extent that it is duplicative of About User data and as hereinafter provided). Upon request by About, ebix shall provide About with About Customer Data in the aggregated form, which aggregated form shall be jointly owned by ebix and About. During the Term and any time thereafter, eBix shall not sell, lease, transfer, disseminate, display or otherwise disclose any About User data to any third party or otherwise use the same for any reason other than as set forth herein. However, ebix shall not be prohibited from selling, leasing, transferring, disseminating, displaying or otherwise disclosing any About Customer Data, as defined in Section 1 above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes financial details from the definition of 'About Customer Data'. The contradicted text obligates Ebix to provide About with About Customer Data in aggregated form. Because financial data is inherently part of customer data, the exclusion in the changed text directly contradicts the obligation to provide aggregated 'About Customer Data' because it creates uncertainty as to whether or not aggregated financial data needs to be shared, leading to a potential dispute. This ambiguity makes the enforcement of Section 9.1 unclear because it is now up to interpretation whether that aggregated data should contain customer financial data.

perturbation.original_text: FILLED APPLICATION FORM" means when an About User accesses an application form for buying insurance through a Link from the Insurance Center and completely fills out all fields of the form and clicks the "Submit" button. The sample format of the application form is enclosed in Exhibit J. If ebix wishes to increase the length of the Filled Application Form by more than 10% during the Term, About must approve such change.
perturbation.changed_text: "FILLED APPLICATION FORM" means when an About User accesses an application form for buying insurance through a Link from the Insurance Center and completely fills out the form, **including only the mandatory fields**, and clicks the "Submit" button. The sample format of the application form is enclosed in Exhibit J. If ebix wishes to increase the length of the Filled Application Form by more than 10% during the Term, About must approve such change.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'completely fills out all fields' is now changed to 'including only the mandatory fields.' This creates an in-text contradiction because section 4.5 states, 'About shall use commercially reasonable efforts to deliver [**] Filled Application Forms per Quarter', but it is now unclear what constitutes a complete application form for the purposes of meeting this goal. Some fields might be viewed as necessary for the application to be accepted for insurance, but are left as optional.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Additionally, About shall use commercially reasonable efforts to deliver [**] Filled Application Forms per Quarter. If, however, at the end of a Quarter, About has not generated the applicable Quarterly Filled Application goals as set forth on EXHIBIT I, About shall deliver additional advertising impressions, in the amounts also set forth on EXHIBIT I.
perturbation.justification: The modification changes the definition of 'FILLED APPLICATION FORM' from requiring all fields to be filled to only mandatory fields. This creates a contradiction with the requirement in the 'Contradicted Text' that About deliver a certain number of 'Filled Application Forms' per quarter because it is now unclear what constitutes a 'Filled Application Form' for purposes of meeting this goal. Some fields that might be viewed as necessary are now optional, leading to ambiguity. The 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition applies here.

perturbation.original_text: CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" means all non-public information concerning either party, its subsidiaries and affiliates, and their respective officers, agents, employees, consultants, licensors, suppliers and ** Confidential treatment has been requested for portions of this document. The redacted material has been filed with the commission pursuant to an application for confidential treatment. 2 customers, including but not limited to business plans, systems configurations, technologies, data files, reports, projections, initiatives, user data and site usage data.
perturbation.changed_text: "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" means all non-public information concerning either party, its subsidiaries and affiliates, and their respective officers, agents, employees, consultants, licensors, suppliers and ** Confidential treatment has been requested for portions of this document. The redacted material has been filed with the commission pursuant to an application for confidential treatment. 2 customers, including but not limited to business plans, systems configurations, technologies, data files, reports, projections, initiatives, user data and site usage data, **except for any data shared with regulatory bodies or required by law to be disclosed**.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'except for any data shared with regulatory bodies or required by law to be disclosed' to the definition of Confidential Information introduces ambiguity. While section 12.1 already addresses legally required disclosures, the blanket exception now potentially clashes with the specific confidentiality obligations outlined in section 12.1, as it is now unclear whether this exception allows for broader disclosures beyond legal requirements, particularly if a regulator requests information that is not strictly mandated by law, this is contradicting the confidentiality agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties may from time to time receive from one another certain information that is proprietary or confidential to the disclosing party, including the About Confidential Information and the ebix Confidential Information (collectively, the "Confidential Information"). The recipient of such Confidential Information shall hold such Confidential Information in confidence, shall not use it except to further its relationship with the other party under this Agreement, and shall not publish or disclose it to third parties unless authorized in writing by the disclosing party. These restrictions shall not apply to any Confidential Information: (i) after it has become generally available to the public without of breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; (ii) is rightfully in the receiving party's possession before disclosure to it by the disclosing party; (iii) is independently developed by the ** Confidential treatment has been requested for portions of this document. The redacted material has been filed with the commission pursuant to an application for confidential treatment. 8 receiving party; (iv) rightfully received the receiving party from a third party without confidentiality; or (v) is required to be disclosed under operation of law or administrative process. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement for any reason, each party will promptly and at the direction of the other party, either destroy or return to the disclosing party, and will not take or use, all items of any nature which belong to the disclosing party and all records (in any form, format or medium) containing or relating to Confidential Information. The parties' obligations under this Section 12 shall survive the termination or non-renewal of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change to the definition of 'Confidential Information' introduces an exception ('except for any data shared with regulatory bodies or required by law to be disclosed') that directly conflicts with the confidentiality obligations outlined in the 'Contradicted Text'. While the 'Contradicted Text' already addresses legally required disclosures (clause (v)), the broad exception in the changed definition creates ambiguity about the extent of permissible disclosures. Specifically, it's unclear whether this new exception allows disclosures beyond legal requirements if a regulator requests information not strictly mandated by law, potentially undermining the confidentiality agreement and the clause to hold information in confidence and not disclose it to third parties unless authorized in writing. This creates a direct conflict and thus a strong in-text contradiction.
